blocks:
Ayahs

أَلَمْ نَشْرَحْ لَكَ صَدْرَكَ (۱)
Did We not open your breast for you (1)
وَوَضَعْنَا عَنكَ وِزْرَكَ (۲)
and relieve you of your burden (2)
ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنقَضَ ظَهْرَكَ (۳)
which [almost] broke your back? (3)
وَرَفَعْنَا لَكَ ذِكْرَكَ (٤)
Did We not exalt your name? (4)
فَإِنَّ مَعَ ٱلْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا (٥)
Indeed ease accompanies hardship. (5)
إِنَّ مَعَ ٱلْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا (٦)
Indeed ease accompanies hardship. (6)
فَإِذَا فَرَغْتَ فَٱنصَبْ (۷)
So when you are done, appoint, (7)
وَإِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ فَٱرْغَب (۸)
and supplicate your Lord. (8)

Key Points

In the previous Surah (Ad-Duha), He said: Allah has not forsaken you: «ما ودّعك ربّك» (Your Lord has not forsaken you). In this Surah, He says: How could He forsake you, when He has granted you the greatest favors and assets, which is the expansion of the breast (capacity): «ألم نشرح لك صدرك» (Did We not expand for you your breast?).

"Sharh Sadr" (expansion of the breast) means the opening of the chest and the raising of human capacity so that one can endure hardships and maintain patience and steadfastness during difficulties and struggles.

When Allah chose Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) for the prophethood, his first request was the expansion of the breast, saying: «ربّ اشرح لى صدرى»(1) (My Lord! Expand for me my breast). But the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) received this favor from Allah without even asking for it. «الم نشرح لك صدرك»

The heavy burden that weighed upon the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله), and which Allah removed from his shoulders by granting him the expansion of the breast, is the responsibility of the prophethood, inviting people to Monotheism, and fighting corruption, polytheism, and superstitions.

Since sorrows and griefs cannot be reduced, capacities must be increased so that one can endure difficulties and not fall short.

The expansion of the breast is a sign of special Divine favor: «فمن یرد اللّه ان یهدیه یشرح صدره للاسلام»(2) (And whomsoever Allah wills to guide, He expands his breast to Islam). Meaning, whomever Allah wills to be guided, He opens his spirit for the acceptance of Islam.

Psychologically, when facing difficulties, one should not focus the entire mind on the problem, but should also pay attention to the ease that follows, which is a Divine promise and tradition. Hardships (Usr) are the cause of ease (Yusr).

The word «مَعَ» (with) might mean "cause," meaning that every hardship contains within itself experiences and constructive elements. «انّ مع العسر یسراً»

The omission of the letter fa (ف) in the second sentence, «فانّ مع العسر یسرا، انّ مع العسر یسرا», indicates that this principle is universal and not exclusive to the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله).

Righteous leaders who face storms of opposition, difficulties, torture, threats, conspiracies, accusations, obstinacy, wars, and so on, need the expansion of the breast more than anything else. «ألم نشرح لك صدرك»

Based on narrations, which have also been decreed by religious authorities (Maraji'), this Surah and Surah Ad-Duha are considered one Surah, similar to Surah Al-Fil and Surah Quraysh. In obligatory prayers, where reading more than one Surah after Surah Al-Fatiha is not permissible, these two Surahs must be read together.

The evidence for the unity of these two Surahs is the correlation between the last Ayah of the previous Surah and the first Ayah of this Surah, which says: «ألم نشرح لك صدرك»

The expansion of the breast granted to the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) caused him not to curse his people despite all the persecutions, and he would say: «اللّهم اهد قومى فانّهم لا یعلمون»(3) (O Allah, guide my people, for they do not know). And during the Conquest of Mecca, by declaring a general amnesty, he said: «لا تثریب علیكم الیوم» (No blame will there be upon you this day), and I have forgiven everyone, just as Joseph (عليه السلام) forgave all his brothers with this phrase.

An example of «وضعنا عنك وزرك و رفعنا لك ذكرك» (And We removed from you your burden, and raised high for you your mention) was that people entered the religion of Allah in crowds: «یدخلون فى دین اللّه افواجاً»(4) (entering the religion of Allah in multitudes). And in the battles of Badr, Khandaq, and Hunayn, Islam was victorious, and even though some were hypocrites or cowards and fled, Islam continued to grow.

In most cases, this heavy burden was removed from the Prophet’s shoulders by Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام). He performed the most courageous operations in Badr, Khaybar, and Uhud.

Of course, in some instances, the Wizr (burden) and heaviness were removed from the shoulders of the Noble Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) through unseen aids, the descent of angels (5), or the casting of fear (6) into the hearts of the enemies.

Allah has honored the name and remembrance of His Prophet. In the Quran, He commanded salutations and blessings upon His Prophet: «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا صلّوا علیه و سلّموا تسلیماً»(7) (O you who have believed, ask [Allah to confer] blessing upon him and grant him peace). He mentioned his name alongside His own Name in many verses: «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا اطیعوا اللّه و اطیعوا الرسول»(8) (O you who have believed, obey Allah and obey the Messenger). In the Adhan (call to prayer), He placed the testimony to his prophethood alongside the testimony to His own Oneness: «اشهد أن لا اله الاّ اللّه و اشهد انّ محمّداً رسول اللّه» (I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, and I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah). And in the Tashahhud (testimony) of every prayer, these two testimonies are repeated.

A woman complained to Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام) about her poor husband not providing maintenance (Nafaqah). The Imam did not imprison the man and said: «انّ مع العسر یُسرا»(9).

(1) Taha, 25. (2) Al-An'am, 125. (3) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 11, p. 298. (4) An-Nasr, 2. (5) Al Imran, 123-125. (6) Al Imran, 151 and Al-Hashr, 2. (7) Al-Ahzab, 56. (8) Muhammad, 33. (9) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The expansion of the breast is the prerequisite for carrying out the heavy programs of the prophethood. «الم نشرح... وضعنا عنك وزرك»

2- Do not worry about the future of the school (of thought/religion). «و رفعنا لك ذكرك»

3- Fighting superstitions and ignorant customs is very heavy. «وزرك»

4- The Prophet's task was back-breaking. «انقض ظهرك»

5- A good name and reputation are necessary for success in leading society. «رفعنا لك ذكرك»

6- The Divine tradition and plan is that ease follows every hardship. «انّ مع العسر یسرا» (In another place in the Quran, we read: «سیجعل اللّه بعد عُسر یسرا»(10) Hardships and difficulties are transient and turn into ease).

Patience and victory are both old friends; Following patience, the turn of victory arrives.

7- After finishing any task or responsibility, a person must be ready to accept another responsibility and another effort and endeavor. «فاذا فرغت فانصب»

8- Efforts must have a Divine direction. «الى ربّك فارغب»

9- Effort and endeavor must be accompanied by sincerity. «فانصب... الى ربّك فارغب»

10- The utmost effort must be employed to turn toward Allah. «فانصب - الى ربك فارغب»

11- Traversing the path of Allah must be done lovingly and willingly. «الى ربّك فارغب»

12- Desires and inclinations are worthy only of Him, not others. «الى ربّك فارغب»

(10) At-Talaq, 7.
